summary:
Today’s Azure news is dominated by AI-assisted developer tooling and data platform updates: Microsoft highlighted GitHub Copilot across the stack, including Copilot coding agent validation tools, GPT-5.3-Codex LTS in GitHub Copilot, Copilot integrations in the MSSQL extension Schema Designer and Data API builder, and general availability of GitHub Copilot in SQL Server Management Studio 22. On the Azure data side, there were multiple Azure SQL announcements—mid-March 2026 GA updates, DiskANN vector index improvements, versionless key support for TDE in Azure SQL Database, SQL MCP Server preview, and Database DevOps in SSMS powered by SQL projects—alongside Azure Red Hat OpenShift managed/workload identity GA and Azure Databricks Lakeflow Connect Free Tier GA.

A second major theme is secure, scalable cloud operations: Azure DevOps warned that authentication tokens should not be treated as stable data contracts, Kubernetes covered securing production debugging, and the Azure Developer CLI added local run/test workflows for AI agents with `azd`. Other notable items include the retirement of the Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure, new .NET MAUI Maps pin clustering support, and Visual Studio Code 1.112.
